item.title: You Have Hit Your Limit
item.description:
If you hit your usage limit, you’ll need to wait for it to reset, upgrade your plan, or purchase extra usage.

This single sentence from Claude documentation reveals one of the fundamental problems of how AI technologies entering our lives, and the motivation.

AI, as an umbrella term, promises far more than text-based tasks or shipping software projects. The impact depends on how we approach it. It makes us faster and more productive yet the cost is that it thinks on our behalf, replacing judgment, memory, and skills. The boost is significant and addictive. It kills sovereignty and independence silently, and the speed at which that reliance develops is staggering.

item.title: Who pays the piper, calls the tune
item.description:
I woke up this morning thinking about the Turkish proverb “Parayı veren düdüğü çalar.” It is used in English as “Who pays the piper, calls the tune.” It’s not the exact word to word translation but the adoption of the idea is the same. Whoever pays, decides.

“Parayı veren düdüğü çalar.”

The Turkish proverb comes from a Nasreddin Hodja anecdote where children ask the Hodja to buy them penny whistles at the market, but only one child actually hands over the money. When he returns with a single whistle and the others protest, he says: “He who pays, plays the whistle.”
